behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint-rule logic over template text (MOCK-MISS: single-function/string-analysis behavior). No OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA: no). No model-output dependence (LLM-DEP: no). Rejected higher level: behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function with templates containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md excerpts and confirm it emits a violation; verify clean templates pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff between template, interpolation, and fixture assembly (MOCK-MISS: yes, cross-module). In-process wiring is sufficient; no real OS/network/subprocess needed beyond local assembly (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a: no). No model-quality dependence (b: no). Rejected Unit: behavior text requires "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness."
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual prompt builder with production interpolation and fixture loaders, then assert assembled prompt text excludes phase-instruction snippets/patterns from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is wiring/source-of-truth: invocation must read plugin/skill files and not hidden constants (module + filesystem interaction). Local filesystem/module integration catches real failure. Rejected Agentic: behavior text focuses on "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" and "rather than hard-coded prompt rules" (provenance/invocation correctness), not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation with real skill file loading, then assert effective context reflects file content and that prompt/test config contains no duplicated hard-coded behavioral rules.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness configurability/state handoff across modules (variant selection + prompt immutability), reproducible with local fixtures/files. No real network/subprocess needed (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a: no). No required model-quality assertion in this behavior (b: no). Rejected Agentic: text is capability to run variants "without changing test prompt text," not correctness of model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness to swap normal vs degraded/missing SKILL.md fixture while keeping exact same user prompt string; assert run setup succeeds and prompt text hash is identical across variants.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on how the real model behavior changes when skill context degrades (LLM-DEP: yes). Stubbed outputs would hide this regression (MOCK-HIDE: yes). Single model decision path, not multi-agent pipeline (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one prompt-response behavioral check, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model twice with identical user-style prompt: once with intact SKILL.md and once with degraded/removed target section. Assert structural behavior passes in intact case and fails in degraded case.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering between capture and parser modules plus artifact write path; local module+filesystem integration is enough. No need for real external infra (REAL-INFRA: no) and no dependence on model-quality variability (LLM-DEP: no). Rejected Unit: ordering bug often appears only when capture hook and parser are wired together.
behaviors.test_description: Inject representative raw completion text, execute harness pipeline, and assert artifact is persisted before parser invocation (e.g., via timestamped hooks/call-order trace) and contains unmodified raw output.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is assertion-engine logic (schema/path/field-position checks) and can fail within one local validator function/object. No infra or model dependence required. Rejected Integration: behavior text targets constraint semantics, not cross-module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted outputs that contain expected keywords but violate schema paths/role field placement; assert structural validator fails while keyword-only helper would pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on actual model outputs, which are nondeterministic/content-dependent (LLM-DEP: yes). Mocked outputs can force pass and hide real misses (MOCK-HIDE: yes). Rejected Integration: text says "Under live model execution," disqualifying stub-only verification.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model smoke test with user-style prompt and compare two evaluators on same raw output: keyword-only vs structural. Assert at least one regression case is caught by structural assertions while keyword-only passes.
